declarations.description:
Group container for `<foundry-details>` children. Discovers items via
`slotchange` + `instanceof` and listens for the native `toggle` event
bubbling up from each child to coordinate open state:

 - `mode="single"` (default): opening one item closes the others.
 - `mode="multiple"`: each item toggles independently; the parent
   provides grouped styling only.

Emits a composed `change` event after any user-initiated toggle with
`event.detail.openValues` — an array of `resolvedValue` strings for
every currently-open item.
declarations.name: FoundryAccordion

members.name: properties
members.default: { mode: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_MODE satisfies AccordionMode }, }

members.name: #coordinating
members.default: false

members.name: items
members.description: Snapshot of discovered `<foundry-details>` children.

events.name: change
events.description: Bubbles + composed. `event.detail.openValues: string[]` is the list of open items' resolvedValues after the change.

attributes.description: Coordination model. Default `single`. Switching `multiple → single` at runtime collapses all but the first open item.
attributes.name: mode

declarations.summary: Grouped disclosure items with single-open or multi-open modes.

declarations.description:
Presentational pill for status and intent. Renders a small rounded
surface coloured from the `--foundry-color-intent-*` semantic tokens.
No ARIA role — the slotted text is the announcement; consumers that need
live-region semantics wrap the badge themselves.
declarations.name: FoundryBadge

cssProperties.description: Pill background. Defaults to the intent's semantic background.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-badge-background

cssProperties.description: Pill text color. Defaults to the intent's semantic foreground.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-badge-foreground

slots.description: The badge content (short label, count, or icon + label).

members.name: properties
members.default: { /** Intent (neutral / info / success / warning / danger). Defaults to `neutral`. */ variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_VARIANT satisfies BadgeVariant, }, }

attributes.description: Intent. Defaults to `neutral`.
attributes.name: variant

declarations.summary: Intent-tier status / count pill.

declarations.description:
A single breadcrumb item. Intended as a child of `<foundry-breadcrumbs>`,
but works independently. The default slot takes the item's content —
usually a `<foundry-link>` or plain text when `current` is set.

When `current` is true, the host gains `aria-current="page"` so assistive
technology announces this item as the current page, and the trailing
separator is hidden. The last item's separator is also hidden via
`:host(:last-of-type)` so trailing punctuation never shows.
declarations.name: FoundryBreadcrumb

cssParts.description: The wrapper around the default slot.
cssParts.name: content

cssParts.description: The wrapper around the separator slot.
cssParts.name: separator

slots.description: The item content (link or plain text).

members.name: properties
members.default: { /** Marks this item as the current page. */ current: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

attributes.description: Marks this item as the current page. Reflects `aria-current="page"` on the host. Note: `has-separator` is an internal CSS hook the component sets based on the `separator` slot's content. Not public API.
attributes.name: current

declarations.summary: A single breadcrumb item with an optional separator.

declarations.description:
Intent-aware callout banner. Renders with a soft-surface background,
matching-hue border, and optional bold title. Live-region role is
derived from variant: `alert` (assertive) for danger/warning,
`status` (polite) for info/success/neutral — per WAI-ARIA.
declarations.name: FoundryAlert

cssProperties.description: Surface color. Defaults to the intent's semantic background.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-alert-background

cssProperties.description: Text color. Defaults to the intent's semantic foreground.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-alert-foreground

cssProperties.description: Border color. Defaults to the intent's semantic foreground (same hue as text).
cssProperties.name: --foundry-alert-border-color

cssProperties.description: Body font size. Defaults to font-size-body.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-alert-font-size

cssProperties.description: Body line height. Defaults to line-height-body.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-alert-line-height

cssParts.description: The outer wrapper that carries background + border.
cssParts.name: container

cssParts.description: The title row; empty-state hidden via CSS.
cssParts.name: title

slots.description: Optional title line. Rendered bold above the body. Hidden when no content is assigned.
slots.name: title

members.default: { /** Intent (neutral / info / success / warning / danger). Defaults to `info`. */ variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_VARIANT satisfies AlertVariant, }, }

declarations.summary: Intent-tier feedback banner with title + body slots.

declarations.description:
Navigation trail wrapper. Renders a semantic `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">`
around an ordered list of `<foundry-breadcrumb>` children. No runtime
coordination between parent and children — each child styles and announces
itself independently. The parent only owns the accessible name + list
structure.
declarations.name: FoundryBreadcrumbs
declarations.summary: Semantic breadcrumb-navigation wrapper.

declarations.description:
Profile image with initials fallback. When `src` is set and the image
loads, the image is shown; otherwise initials are rendered — derived from
`name` automatically (first letter of the first two words, uppercased),
or provided explicitly via the default slot.

The host carries `role="img"` and an `aria-label` — either the explicit
`label` attribute or `name` when `label` is absent. When neither is set,
the avatar is marked `aria-hidden="true"` (decorative).
declarations.name: FoundryAvatar

cssProperties.description: Ring color around the status dot.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-avatar-status-ring

slots.description: Optional explicit initials (1–3 characters). Overrides auto-derive from `name`. Useful for non-Latin scripts or custom glyphs.

attributes.description: Image URL. If the image fails to load, the initials fallback is shown.
attributes.name: src

attributes.description: Full name used to derive initials when no default slot content is provided, and as the fallback accessible label.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Explicit accessible label. Overrides `name` for `aria-label` purposes.
attributes.name: label

attributes.description: Avatar size. Defaults to `md`.
attributes.name: size

attributes.description: Visual shape. Defaults to `circle`.
attributes.name: shape

attributes.description: Optional status dot. When omitted, no dot is rendered. Note: `has-image` is an internal CSS hook the component sets automatically based on image load success. Not public API.
attributes.name: status

declarations.summary: Profile image with initials fallback and optional status dot.

declarations.description:
A native button styled by the foundry design system. Wraps a `<button>`
inside an open shadow root; keyboard, focus, and form semantics are inherited
from the native element.

When `loading` is set, the button renders a centered `<foundry-spinner>`
over its label, forces the inner native button disabled (so clicks are
suppressed), and sets `aria-busy="true"` for assistive tech. The label
stays in the DOM (visually hidden only) so the button's width doesn't jump.
declarations.name: FoundryButton

cssProperties.name: --foundry-button-background

cssProperties.description: Background when hovering the primary variant.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-button-background-hover

cssProperties.description: Background when activating the primary variant.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-button-background-active

cssProperties.description: Foreground color for primary and danger variants.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-button-foreground

cssParts.description: The span wrapping the slotted label.
cssParts.name: label

cssParts.description: The absolutely-positioned spinner overlay (only rendered when `loading`).
cssParts.name: spinner

slots.description: Default slot for the button label.

members.name: properties
members.default: { /** Visual variant: `primary` (default), `secondary`, or `danger`. */ variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: 'primary' satisfies ButtonVariant }, /** Whether the button is disabled. Forwarded to the inner native `<button>`. */ disabled: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, /** Whether the button is in a loading state. */ loading: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, /** Native button `type`: `button` (default), `submit`, or `reset`. */ type: { type: String, reflect: true, default: 'button' satisfies ButtonType }, }

members.name: delegatesFocus
members.default: true

attributes.name: variant

attributes.name: disabled

attributes.description: Whether the button is in a loading state. When true, a spinner overlays the label, clicks are suppressed, and the inner button exposes `aria-busy="true"`.

attributes.name: type

declarations.summary: Themable button action element.

declarations.description:
Themed surface container with four optional regions: `media`, `header`,
default (body), and `footer`. Each region auto-hides when its slot is
empty, driven by `has-*` host attributes observed via `slotchange`.

The media region is edge-to-edge (no side padding) so slotted images
sit flush with the card's rounded corners. Header and footer nestle
tight against the body so vertical rhythm stays consistent whether or
not every region is present.

Two visual variants:
 - `outlined` (default) — subtle border, no shadow.
 - `elevated` — no border, drop shadow.

Card is purely presentational. Interactive behavior (click-through,
links) lives in child elements, following the library's convention
that no component's host acts as a button or link.
declarations.name: FoundryCard

cssProperties.description: Inner padding for header / body / footer.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-card-padding

slots.description: Optional header row — typically a `<foundry-heading>` and/or metadata.
slots.name: header

slots.description: Optional edge-to-edge media region above the header (images, charts, illustrations).
slots.name: media

slots.description: Optional action row (buttons, links, secondary content).
slots.name: footer

members.name: properties
members.default: { variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_VARIANT satisfies CardVariant, }, }

attributes.description: Visual treatment. Defaults to `outlined`. Reflects.
attributes.name: variant

declarations.summary: Themed surface container with header / media / body / footer.

declarations.description:
Themable boolean form control. First form control in the library whose
submitted value is not a simple synced string: when unchecked, the field
is **omitted** from `FormData` — matching native `<input type=checkbox>`
semantics. Participates in HTML forms via `ElementInternals`
(`static formAssociated = true`).

The checkbox wraps its inner `<input>` in a native `<label>` element, so
clicking the visual box OR the slotted label text toggles the state —
the browser's native label-click-to-toggle behaviour is preserved even
across the shadow DOM boundary because the slotted content is a
descendant of the `<label>` in the composed tree.
declarations.name: FoundryCheckbox

cssParts.description: The native `<input type="checkbox">`. Visually hidden but focusable + keyboard-interactive.
cssParts.name: input

cssParts.description: The visual checkbox box (shows the check SVG when checked).
cssParts.name: box

cssParts.description: The span wrapping the slotted label text.
cssParts.name: label

members.name: formAssociated
members.default: true

members.name: delegatesFocus
members.default: true

members.name: #applyingUserInput
members.default: false

attributes.description: Form field name. Required for form participation.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Submitted as the field value when checked. Defaults to `on` (matches native `<input type=checkbox>`).
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Current state. Two-way synced with inner input.
attributes.name: checked

attributes.description: Marks the field as required. Unchecked + required reports `valueMissing`.
attributes.name: required

attributes.description: Reflected. Present whenever the inner input's validity fails. Managed by the component; not set by consumers. Note: `has-label` is an internal CSS hook the component sets automatically based on slot content. Not public API.
attributes.name: invalid

declarations.summary: Labelled boolean form control with form association.

declarations.description:
Horizontal layout primitive. Arranges slotted children in a flex row with
consistent spacing pulled from the `--foundry-space-inline-*` tokens.
Wraps to new rows by default; override via `--foundry-cluster-wrap`.
Sibling to `<foundry-stack>` on the inline axis.
declarations.name: FoundryCluster

members.name: properties
members.default: { /** Horizontal gap between children (xs / sm / md / lg). Defaults to `md`. */ space: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_SPACE satisfies ClusterSpace }, }

attributes.description: Horizontal gap between children. Defaults to `md`.
attributes.name: space

declarations.summary: Space-tier horizontal cluster primitive with configurable gap.

declarations.description:
Themable disclosure wrapper around the native `<details>` / `<summary>`
elements. The browser handles expand/collapse toggling, keyboard
(Enter / Space on the summary), the `open` attribute, the `toggle`
event, focus semantics, and screen-reader announcements for free. This
component adds:

- Consistent theming via CSS parts + custom properties.
- A chevron that rotates 180° on open, with `prefers-reduced-motion`
  halting the transition.
- A `disabled` attribute that blocks click + keyboard interaction and
  rejects programmatic `open` writes.
- A `value` attribute + `resolvedValue` getter used by
  `<foundry-accordion>` to report which items are open.

The host `open` attribute is two-way synced with the native
`<details>` via the browser's `toggle` event.
declarations.name: FoundryDetails

slots.description: The clickable title. Rendered inside the native `<summary>`.
slots.name: summary

members.name: properties
members.default: { value: { type: String, reflect: true }, open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, disabled: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: #applyingNativeState
members.default: false

members.name: resolvedValue
members.description:
The details' identifier: the `value` attribute when set, otherwise the
trimmed summary slot text.

attributes.description: Identifier surfaced in the accordion's change event. Defaults to the trimmed `summary`-slot text.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Reflected. Tracks the native details open state.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description: Reflected. Blocks summary interaction and rejects `open` writes when true.
attributes.name: disabled

declarations.summary: Themable disclosure (expand/collapse) built on native `<details>`.

declarations.description:
Presentational rule that separates sections of content. Renders as a 1px
line drawn from the `--foundry-color-border` semantic token by default.
Carries `role="separator"`; `aria-orientation` is emitted only when
vertical (horizontal is the implicit default for the role).
declarations.name: FoundryDivider

declarations.summary: Color-tier horizontal / vertical separator.

declarations.description:
A semantic heading with a visual size that can be chosen independently of
the document-outline level. The host element carries `role="heading"` and
`aria-level` so assistive technology maps it correctly regardless of where
it appears in the DOM. Slotted children render as the heading's text.

declarations.description:
Padding wrapper primitive. Applies uniform padding around slotted content,
pulled from the `--foundry-space-inset-*` tokens. Completes the space-tier
trilogy alongside `<foundry-stack>` (vertical gap) and
`<foundry-cluster>` (horizontal gap).
declarations.name: FoundryInset

attributes.description: Uniform padding rung. Defaults to `md`.
attributes.name: space

declarations.summary: Space-tier padding wrapper with configurable inset.

declarations.description:
Themable hyperlink primitive. Wraps a native `<a>` so keyboard, focus,
middle-click, drag-to-bookmark, and right-click-open-in-new-tab all behave
natively. When `target="_blank"` is set without an explicit `rel`, the
component adds `rel="noopener"` for tab-nap / reverse-tabnabbing safety.

Two variants: `inline` (default, underlined — for links sitting in prose)
and `standalone` (no underline until hover — for nav lists, card titles).
declarations.name: FoundryLink

cssProperties.description: Visited link color. Defaults to `--foundry-link-color` (no distinct visited style).
cssProperties.name: --foundry-link-color-visited

cssProperties.description: Corner radius used for the focus ring only.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-link-focus-radius

members.default: { /** Visual variant: `inline` (default, underlined) or `standalone`. */ variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_VARIANT satisfies LinkVariant }, /** Target URL. */ href: { type: String, reflect: true }, /** Link target (`_self` / `_blank` / `_parent` / `_top`). */ target: { type: String, reflect: true }, /** Relationship tokens. When set, suppresses auto `noopener` on `_blank`. */ rel: { type: String, reflect: true }, /** Download hint. */ download: { type: String, reflect: true }, /** Target-resource language hint. */ hreflang: { type: String, reflect: true }, /** MIME type hint. */ type: { type: String, reflect: true }, }

members.name: delegatesFocus
members.default: true

attributes.name: variant

attributes.name: href

attributes.name: target

declarations.description:
Action menu — a popover surface of `<foundry-menuitem>` children anchored
to a consumer-slotted trigger. Clicking (or Enter/Space-activating) an
item fires a bubbling + composed `select` event with `detail.value` and
auto-closes the menu; consumers can `preventDefault()` the event to keep
the menu open (e.g. multi-select filter menus).

Behavior mirrors the WAI-ARIA menu pattern:
 - Click the trigger to open; click outside (browser light-dismiss) or
   press Escape (browser light-dismiss) to close.
 - Arrow keys move the active descendant (skipping disabled items);
   Home/End jump to first/last.
 - Enter / Space on the focused trigger open the menu (seeded on the
   first enabled item); the same keys invoke the active item once open.
declarations.name: FoundryMenu

slots.description: The trigger element. First assigned element is wired; additional slotted content is ignored for interaction.

members.name: properties
members.default: { open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, placement: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_PLACEMENT satisfies PopoverPlacement, }, }

members.name: #wasOpenAtPointerdown
members.default: false

members.name: #suppressNextTriggerClick
members.default: false

members.name: items
members.description: Snapshot of discovered `<foundry-menuitem>` children.

members.name: show
members.description: Open the menu. No-op if already open.

members.name: hide
members.description: Close the menu. No-op if already closed.

members.name: toggle
members.description: Flip the menu open/closed.

events.description: Bubbles + composed. `event.detail.value` is the invoked item's resolvedValue. Cancelable: `preventDefault()` suppresses the auto-close.
events.name: select

attributes.description: Reflected. Tracks popover open state; managed by the component + browser light-dismiss.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description: Preferred anchoring side. Default `bottom`.
attributes.name: placement

declarations.summary:
Action menu with popover surface, keyboard navigation, and
`select` event dispatch.

declarations.description:
A single action item inside a `<foundry-menu>`. Dispatches actions, not
values — menuitems have no "selected" state (contrast with
`<foundry-option>` which carries `selected` as persistent form state).

Ships three slots aligned on a CSS grid so mixed-content rows stay
visually consistent:
 - `icon` — optional leading icon.
 - default — the label.
 - `shortcut` — optional right-aligned keyboard hint (purely visual;
   the menu does NOT intercept global keystrokes).

The parent `<foundry-menu>` toggles `active` when the keyboard or
pointer highlights this item.
declarations.name: FoundryMenuitem

cssParts.description: The item container (grid: icon | label | shortcut).
cssParts.name: item

members.name: properties
members.default: { value: { type: String, reflect: true }, disabled: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, active: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: resolvedValue
members.description:
The item's identifier: the `value` attribute when set, otherwise the
trimmed default-slot text.

attributes.description: Identifier surfaced in the menu's `select` event. Defaults to the trimmed default-slot text.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Reflected. Disabled items are keyboard-skipped and click-inert.
attributes.name: disabled

attributes.description: Reflected. Managed by the parent menu; drives the keyboard/pointer highlight.
attributes.name: active

declarations.summary: A single action item in a `<foundry-menu>`.

declarations.description:
Accessible modal dialog with form-close integration. Wraps a native
`<dialog>` element so the browser handles the hard parts for free:
top-layer hoisting, modal focus containment, inert background, and the
`::backdrop` pseudo-element. The component adds three ergonomic layers
on top:

 1. **Slotted header/body/footer** with stable IDs and dynamic
    `aria-labelledby` / `aria-describedby` wiring.
 2. **Triple dismiss model** — Escape (native), backdrop click (opt-out
    via `dismiss-on-backdrop=false`), and a built-in close button in the
    header (opt-out via `hide-close-button`).
 3. **Form-close integration** — a `<form method="dialog">` inside the
    body automatically closes the dialog on submit and sets
    `returnValue` to the clicked button's `value`, surfaced on the
    host's bubbling+composed `close` event as `event.detail.returnValue`.
declarations.name: FoundryModal

cssParts.description: The icon inside the close button.
cssParts.name: close-icon

slots.description: Default slot. The main body content.

members.name: properties
members.default: { open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, size: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_SIZE satisfies ModalSize }, dismissOnBackdrop: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: true }, hideCloseButton: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: #applyingNativeState
members.default: false

members.name: show
members.description: Open the modal via the native `showModal()`. Idempotent.

members.name: close
members.description: Close the modal. Sets the dialog's `returnValue` if provided. Idempotent.

members.name: returnValue
members.description: Proxies the native dialog's returnValue.

events.description: Bubbles + composed. `event.detail.returnValue` mirrors the dialog's returnValue.
events.name: close

events.description: The native dialog cancel event bubbles through (Escape keypress). Consumers can `preventDefault()` to veto.
events.name: cancel

attributes.description: Reflected. Tracks dialog open state. Managed by the component + the browser's native close (Escape, form submit, backdrop click). Consumers read for styling; prefer `show()` / `close()` to write.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description: Width tier. Defaults to `md`. Overridable with `--foundry-modal-max-inline-size`.
attributes.name: size

attributes.description: When `true` (default), clicking outside the dialog body closes it with `returnValue='dismiss'`. Set to `false` for blocking modals.
attributes.name: dismiss-on-backdrop

attributes.description: Hides the built-in close button in the header.
attributes.name: hide-close-button

declarations.summary: Accessible dialog backed by the native `<dialog>` element.

declarations.description:
A single selectable option. Intended as a child of `<foundry-select>`,
but works independently. Carries a `value` (the submitted data) and a
trimmed `textContent` (the visible label). The parent `<foundry-select>`
toggles `selected` when its own `value` matches this option.

In Phase 1 of `<foundry-select>`, options are inert — they render as list
rows inside the listbox surface but the listbox never opens. Phase 2 will
wire click-to-select + keyboard navigation and add `:host([selected])` /
`:host([active])` styling hooks.
declarations.name: FoundryOption

members.name: properties
members.default: { value: { type: String, reflect: true }, disabled: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, selected: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, active: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: resolvedValue
members.description:
The option's submitted value: the `value` attribute when set, otherwise
the trimmed `textContent`. Used by the parent `<foundry-select>` to
resolve a matching option when its own `value` changes.

attributes.description: The option's submitted value. Defaults to the trimmed `textContent` when omitted.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Reflected. Disabled options can't be selected.
attributes.name: disabled

attributes.description: Reflected. Managed by the parent select; not meant for consumer writes.
attributes.name: selected

attributes.name: active

declarations.summary: A selectable item inside a `<foundry-select>` listbox.

declarations.description:
Controlled page selector. Renders a `<nav>` with a prev button,
numbered page buttons (with ellipsis truncation), and a next button.
Consumers own the `page` attribute; the component emits a `change`
event with `detail.page` when the user activates a page, and the
consumer writes the new value back.

Truncation rule: always show page 1 and page `total`, plus
`siblingCount` neighbors on each side of the current page, with
ellipses filling the gaps. Totals ≤ 7 render every page.

Prev/next are always present; they're `disabled` at page 1 and page
`total` respectively. Keyboard: Arrow-Left/Right move focus across
enabled buttons; Home/End jump to first/last; Enter / Space / click
activate the focused button.
declarations.name: FoundryPagination

cssParts.description: The currently-active page button (carries `aria-current="page"`, in addition to the `page` part).
cssParts.name: page-current

type.text: { page: number; total: number; siblingCount: number }

events.name: change

attributes.name: total

attributes.name: sibling-count

declarations.description:
A single tab panel. Intended as a child of `<foundry-tabs>`, auto-paired
with a `<foundry-tab>` at the same index. Renders its slotted content
when `selected` is true; hidden otherwise.

The parent `<foundry-tabs>` wires `aria-labelledby` to the matching
tab's ID so assistive tech announces "{tab-label}, tab panel" when the
panel receives focus.
declarations.name: FoundryPanel

members.name: properties
members.default: { selected: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

attributes.description: Reflected. Managed by the parent tabs; not meant for consumer writes. Controls visibility + `aria-hidden`.
attributes.name: selected

declarations.summary: A tab panel paired with a `<foundry-tab>` at the same index.

declarations.description:
Click-to-toggle light-dismiss popover. Wraps a trigger element (default
slot) and renders arbitrary content (`content` slot) in the top layer via
the HTML Popover API (`popover="auto"`). Light-dismiss is browser-native:
outside click + Escape close the popover; opening another `auto` popover
closes this one.

The trigger is wired via the `popovertarget` attribute — browsers handle
the full show/hide cycle (including the re-open-on-click-while-open
corner case). We listen to the surface's `toggle` event to keep
`aria-expanded` on the trigger in sync. The `PopoverController`
independently keeps the host's `open` attribute in sync for styling.
declarations.name: FoundryPopover

slots.description: The trigger element. One element; gets `popovertarget`, `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, `aria-controls`, and `aria-expanded` wired.

members.default: { placement: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_PLACEMENT satisfies PopoverPlacement, }, open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: #wasOpenAtPointerdown
members.default: false

attributes.description: Preferred side. Defaults to `bottom`. No auto-flip in this version.
attributes.name: placement

attributes.description: Reflected. Managed by the component + the browser's light-dismiss; consumers read for styling but shouldn't set directly.
attributes.name: open

declarations.summary: Click-to-toggle light-dismiss popover anchored to a trigger.

declarations.description:
Themable radio form control. Exactly one radio per `name` group can be
checked at a time. Radios self-coordinate: when one becomes checked it
queries siblings via `form.elements.namedItem(name)` (or a document-level
query for orphan radios) and unchecks them.

Arrow keys move focus + selection between siblings per the WAI-ARIA
radiogroup pattern; roving tabindex ensures only one radio per group is
in the tab order.

Participates in HTML forms via `ElementInternals`. Only the checked
radio in a group surfaces `name=value` in `FormData`; unchecked radios
are omitted (matches native `<input type=radio>` semantics).
declarations.name: FoundryRadio

declarations.description:
Determinate progress indicator. The host carries `role="progressbar"` plus
`aria-valuenow` / `aria-valuemin` / `aria-valuemax`, so assistive tech
announces progress without any wrapper. A CSS-animated bar fills the
track from 0 to 100% of `(value / max)`; the fill transition is halted
under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.

Values are clamped to `[0, max]`; non-finite values fall back to `0`.
declarations.name: FoundryProgress

members.name: #syncAria

attributes.name: max

attributes.name: variant

attributes.name: label

declarations.summary: Determinate progress bar with a clamped value and intent variants.

declarations.description:
Themable select form control. Renders a dropdown-style trigger that
displays the current selection's label (or a placeholder when none) and
a listbox surface opened on click / keyboard. Participates in HTML forms
via `ElementInternals` (`static formAssociated = true`).

Children are `<foundry-option>` elements; the select discovers them via
`slotchange` on the listbox's default slot. `value` is the source of
truth — options reflect `selected` based on whether their
`resolvedValue` matches.

**Open behavior.** The listbox uses the HTML Popover API
(`popover="auto"`) so light-dismiss is browser-native: outside click and
Escape close it, and another `auto` popover pre-empts this one. The
`PopoverController` positions the surface with flip+shift, keeping the
host's `open` attribute in sync.

**Keyboard (WAI-ARIA listbox pattern).**
  - Closed: Enter / Space / ArrowDown / ArrowUp / Home / End → open.
  - Open: Arrow keys move the active descendant (skipping disabled
    options); Home / End jump to first / last; printable keys start a
    typeahead search (resets after 500 ms of inactivity); Enter / Space
    commit the active option; Escape closes without committing.

Active descendant is tracked via `aria-activedescendant` on the trigger
so focus stays on the trigger — the listbox surface is never tabbed
through, matching native `<select>` and the WAI-ARIA APG listbox pattern.
declarations.name: FoundrySelect

cssParts.description: Outer flex wrapper (label + control + hint/error + listbox).
cssParts.name: container

cssParts.description: The popover surface containing slotted options.
cssParts.name: listbox

cssParts.description: The error row (visible only when invalid).
cssParts.name: error

slots.name: label

slots.name: hint

Low-contrast placeholder for content that's still loading. Decorative by
default (`aria-hidden="true"`). Setting `label` promotes the host to
`role="status"` with `aria-label`, mirroring `<foundry-spinner>`.

The pulse animation honors `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — the
placeholder still renders at the same size, but the opacity loop stops.

(`aria-hidden="true"`). Setting `label` promotes the host to

announcement that a loading state is active.

The spin animation honors `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — the arc is
still visible, but rotation stops.

declarations.description:
A single tab in a tablist. Intended as a child of `<foundry-tabs>`, but
works independently. Carries a `value` (the identifier used to resolve
which panel this tab activates) and a visible label via slotted content.
The parent `<foundry-tabs>` toggles `selected` when its own `value`
matches this tab.

Auto-pairs with a `<foundry-panel>` at the same index by default; the
parent generates stable IDs and wires `aria-controls` / `aria-labelledby`
so the tab and its matching panel cross-reference each other.
declarations.name: FoundryTab

The tab's identifier: the `value` attribute when set, otherwise the
trimmed `textContent`. Used by the parent `<foundry-tabs>` to resolve
a matching tab when its own `value` changes.

attributes.name: value

attributes.name: disabled

attributes.name: selected

declarations.description:
Themable toggle form control. Semantically a setting that takes effect
immediately (as opposed to a checkbox, which commits on submit). Built on
an inner `<input type="checkbox">` with `role="switch"` so assistive tech
announces it as a toggle, while keeping native form association,
constraint validation, and keyboard interactions (Space toggles) for free.

Participates in HTML forms via `ElementInternals` (`static formAssociated
= true`). When checked, submits `name=value`; when unchecked, the field is
**omitted** from `FormData` — matching native `<input type=checkbox>`
semantics.

The switch wraps its inner `<input>` in a native `<label>` element, so
clicking the visual track OR the slotted label text toggles the state —
the browser's native label-click-to-toggle behaviour is preserved even
across the shadow DOM boundary because the slotted content is a
descendant of the `<label>` in the composed tree.
declarations.name: FoundrySwitch

cssParts.description: The native `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">`. Visually hidden but focusable + keyboard-interactive.
cssParts.name: input

cssParts.description: The sliding circle inside the track.
cssParts.name: thumb

cssParts.description: The span wrapping the slotted label text.
cssParts.name: label

declarations.description:
Dismissible intent pill. A sibling of `<foundry-badge>` that can be
removed via an inline close button. When `removable`, clicking or pressing
Enter/Space on the close button dispatches a cancelable `remove` event.
If the listener does NOT call `preventDefault()`, the tag removes itself
from the DOM — giving vanilla consumers working UX with zero code. If
the listener prevents the default, the tag stays mounted and the consumer
handles state.
declarations.name: FoundryTag

declarations.description:
Pure helper exported for unit testing — returns the explicit `value`
attribute when non-empty, otherwise the trimmed slotted label.

Accessible tabbed interface. Parent component that coordinates a set of
`<foundry-tab>` children (in the `tab` slot) with `<foundry-panel>`
children (in the default slot). Tabs and panels are auto-paired by DOM
order — the first tab activates the first panel, the second tab the
second panel, and so on.

The component implements the WAI-ARIA APG **tabs pattern with manual
activation**: Arrow keys move focus between tabs but do not activate
them; Enter, Space, or click activates the focused tab. This pattern
is preferred when panel content is non-trivial to render (network
fetches, heavy DOM).

Keyboard axis flips with `orientation`: horizontal uses Arrow Left /
Right; vertical uses Arrow Up / Down. Home / End always jump to the
first / last enabled tab. Disabled tabs are skipped.

Stable IDs are generated per instance so assistive tech can
cross-reference each tab with its panel via `aria-controls` and
`aria-labelledby`. Consumer-supplied IDs are preserved.
declarations.name: FoundryTabs

declarations.description:
Themable text field with built-in label, hint, and error slots. Participates
in HTML forms via `ElementInternals` (`static formAssociated = true`), so
submission captures `name`/`value` just like a native `<input>`. Constraint
validation is delegated to the inner input — set `required`, `pattern`,
`minlength`, etc. on the host and the component mirrors the input's
`validity` onto `internals.setValidity(...)`. When the input becomes
invalid, the host reflects `invalid` so CSS can style the border red
using the intent-danger tokens.
declarations.name: FoundryTextField

declarations.description:
Transient notification. Designed to live inside a
`<foundry-toast-region>`, though it works standalone.

Behavior:
 - Variant drives `role="status"` (info / success / neutral) vs
   `role="alert"` (warning / danger), matching `<foundry-alert>`.
 - Auto-dismisses after `duration` ms (default 5000). `duration="0"`
   disables auto-dismiss (manual close only).
 - Pointer hover and focus-within pause the timer; pointerleave /
   focusout resume from the remaining time.
 - Escape on the focused toast triggers dismiss (close-button reason).
 - `dismiss` event is cancelable — `preventDefault()` keeps the toast
   alive (useful for dirty-state guards on action toasts).
 - `close` event fires after the toast is removed from the DOM; the
   imperative handle returned by `region.add()` awaits this.
declarations.name: FoundryToast

cssProperties.description: Grid gap between icon / content / close.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-toast-gap

slots.description: Body content (plain text or rich markup).

members.name: properties
members.default: { variant: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_VARIANT satisfies ToastVariant, }, duration: { type: Number, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_DURATION }, closeable: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: true }, open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: #paused
members.default: false

members.name: #dismissing
members.default: false

members.name: #openEventFired
members.default: false

members.name: closed
members.description:
Promise that resolves after the toast's `close` event fires. The
imperative handle returned by `region.add()` forwards this so
consumers can `await region.add(...).closed`.

members.name: dismiss
members.description:
Programmatically dismiss the toast. Dispatches the cancelable
`dismiss` event; if not prevented, removes the toast from the DOM
and dispatches `close`.

events.name: close
events.description: Bubbles + composed. Fires after removal from the DOM.
Bubbles + composed + cancelable. Fires before removal. `event.detail.reason: 'timeout' | 'close-button' | 'manual'`.
events.name: dismiss

attributes.description: Intent. Drives `role` and surface tokens. Defaults to `info`.
attributes.name: variant

attributes.description: Auto-dismiss in milliseconds. `0` disables. Defaults to 5000.
attributes.name: duration

attributes.description: Shows the close button. Defaults to `true`.
attributes.name: closeable

attributes.description: Reflected. Present while the toast is live; removed when `dismiss()` starts.
attributes.name: open

declarations.summary: A single transient notification.

declarations.description:
Themable multi-line text field with built-in label, hint, and error
slots. Near-clone of `<foundry-text-field>`, wrapping a `<textarea>`
instead of `<input>`. Participates in HTML forms via `ElementInternals`
(`static formAssociated = true`), so submission captures `name`/`value`
just like a native `<textarea>`. Constraint validation is delegated to
the inner element — set `required`, `minlength`, `maxlength` on the
host and the component mirrors the textarea's `validity` onto
`internals.setValidity(...)`. When invalid, the host reflects `invalid`
so CSS can style the border red using the intent-danger tokens.
declarations.name: FoundryTextarea

cssParts.description: The `<label>` element (linked to the textarea via aria-labelledby).
cssParts.name: label

cssParts.description: The native `<textarea>` element. Named `input` for consistency with `<foundry-text-field>` so ::part(input) works across both controls.
cssParts.name: input

cssParts.description: The error container (visible only when invalid).
cssParts.name: error

slots.description: Optional helper copy shown below the textarea.
slots.name: hint

slots.description: Optional error message shown only when the field is invalid.
slots.name: error

members.name: formAssociated
members.default: true

members.name: properties
members.default: { name: { type: String, reflect: true }, value: { type: String, reflect: true, default: '' }, placeholder: { type: String, reflect: true }, required: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, disabled: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, readonly: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, minlength: { type: Number, reflect: true }, maxlength: { type: Number, reflect: true }, autocomplete: { type: String, reflect: true }, inputmode: { type: String, reflect: true }, rows: { type: Number, reflect: true, default: 3 }, invalid: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

members.name: delegatesFocus
members.default: true

members.name: #applyingUserInput
members.default: false

attributes.description: Form field name. Submitted with the form.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Current value. Two-way synced with the inner textarea.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Placeholder text.
attributes.name: placeholder

attributes.description: Marks the field as required for validation.
attributes.name: required

attributes.description: Disables the inner textarea.
attributes.name: disabled

attributes.description: Makes the inner textarea readonly.
attributes.name: readonly

attributes.description: Minimum allowed length.
attributes.name: minlength

attributes.description: Maximum allowed length.
attributes.name: maxlength

attributes.description: Autocomplete hint forwarded to the textarea.
attributes.name: autocomplete

attributes.description: Virtual-keyboard hint forwarded to the textarea.
attributes.name: inputmode

attributes.description: Visible row count on the inner textarea. Defaults to `3`.
attributes.name: rows

attributes.description: Reflected. Present whenever the inner textarea's validity fails. Managed by the component; not set by consumers. Note: `has-label`, `has-hint`, and `has-error` are internal CSS hooks the component sets automatically based on slot content. Not public API.
attributes.name: invalid

declarations.summary: Labelled multi-line text field with hint, error, and form association.

declarations.description:
Long-lived container that manages a stack of `<foundry-toast>` items.
Consumers typically create one at app-shell root and spawn toasts
imperatively via `region.add({ variant, message })`, which returns a
handle:

  const handle = region.add({ variant: 'success', message: 'Saved' });
  await handle.closed;  // resolves after the toast leaves the DOM

Declarative `<foundry-toast slot="items">` children work too — they
participate in the same stack and `max` enforcement.

The region is anchored to one of six viewport corners via the
`position` attribute (logical-property-aware, so RTL flips start / end).
declarations.name: FoundryToastRegion

cssProperties.description: Outer padding from the viewport edge.
cssProperties.name: --foundry-toast-region-padding

slots.description: Holds `<foundry-toast>` children (imperatively added or declared in markup).
slots.name: items

members.name: properties
members.default: { position: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_POSITION satisfies ToastPosition, }, max: { type: Number, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_MAX }, }

members.name: #enforcing
members.default: false

members.name: toasts
members.description: Snapshot of currently-mounted `<foundry-toast>` children.

members.name: add
members.description:
Spawn a new toast from options. Returns a handle with
`toast` (the DOM element), `dismiss(reason?)` (a Promise that
resolves after removal), and `closed` (a Promise that resolves when
the toast's `close` event fires).

attributes.description: Viewport anchoring. One of `top-start`, `top-center`, `top-end`, `bottom-start`, `bottom-center`, `bottom-end`. Default `bottom-end`. Reflects.
attributes.name: position

attributes.description: Cap on visible toasts. When exceeded, oldest toasts auto-dismiss with `reason: 'timeout'`. Default 5.
attributes.name: max

declarations.summary: Region manager for transient notifications.

declarations.description:
Accessible tooltip. Wraps a trigger element (default slot) and renders a
tooltip in the top layer via the HTML Popover API (`popover="manual"`).
Shows on hover (after `delay-show`) or focus (immediate); hides on
leave/blur. Accessible name is wired via `aria-describedby` on the
trigger → `role="tooltip"` surface.

Positioning, top-layer plumbing, and viewport-change handling are all
delegated to the shared `PopoverController` in `core/` — the same
primitive every future popover-adjacent component will use.
declarations.name: FoundryTooltip

cssParts.description: The CSS-triangle arrow pointing at the trigger.
cssParts.name: arrow

slots.description: The trigger element. The first assigned element is wired as the anchor. Additional slotted elements are ignored for interaction but still render in the light DOM.
The tooltip body (text or simple inline content).
slots.name: content

members.name: properties
members.default: { placement: { type: String, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_PLACEMENT satisfies PopoverPlacement, }, delayShow: { type: Number, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_DELAY_SHOW }, delayHide: { type: Number, reflect: true, default: DEFAULT_DELAY_HIDE }, open: { type: Boolean, reflect: true, default: false }, }

attributes.description: Preferred side. No auto-flip in this version. Defaults to `top`.
attributes.name: placement

attributes.description: Milliseconds to wait before showing on hover. Focus-show is always immediate. Default 300.
attributes.name: delay-show

attributes.description: Milliseconds to wait before hiding. Default 0.
attributes.name: delay-hide

attributes.description: Reflected internal state. Managed by the component; consumers read for styling but shouldn't set directly.
attributes.name: open

declarations.summary: Accessible hover/focus tooltip with top-layer rendering.
